skill.name: image-inpainting
skill.description: Mask-driven image inpainting on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI. Routes to Z-Image Turbo Inpainting and description-based edit models for object removal, watermark removal, and region replacement.
skill.summary: Mask-driven image inpainting skill that routes to RunComfy models for object removal, watermark cleanup, and region replacement.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 103 patterns (backticks, URLs, crypto terms) but all are false positives. The file is a SKILL.md documentation file with markdown code blocks showing bash CLI invocations, legitimate documentation URLs to runcomfy.com, and a well-written Security & Privacy section. The 'hidden file' reference is advice about token storage in ~/.config/runcomfy/. No executable code, no malicious intent detected.

content.user_title: Remove Objects and Edit Image Regions with AI
content.value_statement: Mask-driven image inpainting removes unwanted objects, replaces backgrounds, and fills masked regions using AI models. This skill routes your edits to the optimal RunComfy model for precise or description-based results.
actual_capabilities: Route inpainting tasks to Z-Image Turbo Inpainting when a binary mask is provided
Fall back to description-based edit models (Nano Banana 2, GPT Image 2, FLUX Kontext) when no mask exists
Remove objects, watermarks, and blemishes from images via CLI
Replace masked regions with new content using strength and control-scale parameters
Support LoRA-style brand-locked inpainting for consistent visual identity
Output results to a specified directory with proper exit code handling
limitations: Requires the runcomfy CLI to be installed and authenticated
Mask-driven route requires user-provided grayscale mask URLs
Cannot perform outpainting (canvas extension) or video inpainting
Source images and masks from untrusted URLs may contain indirect prompt injection

use_cases.title: Remove Watermarks from Product Photos
use_cases.description: E-commerce sellers can clean watermark overlays from stock images before listing products. Provide a mask around the watermark and the AI fills the region seamlessly.

use_cases.title: Clean Up Street Photography
use_cases.description: Photographers can remove overhead cables, tourists, or distracting elements from travel photos. Use a low strength setting to blend fills naturally with surroundings.
use_cases.target_user: Travel photographers and visual content creators

use_cases.title: Replace Photo Backgrounds for Listings
use_cases.description: Real estate agents and car dealerships can swap busy backgrounds for clean studio backdrops. Mask the background and describe the replacement for full-region swaps.
use_cases.target_user: Real estate agents and automotive marketers

prompt_templates.title: Basic Object Removal
prompt_templates.prompt: Remove the [object] from this image. Mask is provided covering the [object] region. Preserve the surrounding scene exactly as-is.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - removing a single unwanted element

prompt_templates.title: Watermark Cleanup
prompt_templates.prompt: Remove the watermark in the [location] corner. Use strength 0.5 with the provided mask. Keep everything else exactly as in the input image.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - watermark removal with controlled strength

prompt_templates.title: Background Replacement
prompt_templates.prompt: Mask covers the entire background. Replace with [description of new background]. Use strength 0.9 for full replacement. Match lighting direction of the foreground subject.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - full background swap for product photography

prompt_templates.title: Brand-Style Locked Inpainting
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply brand LoRA to inpainting endpoint. Mask defines region. Prompt must specify both the fill content and the brand style constraints. Use Z-Image Inpainting LoRA variant.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced - LoRA-based brand consistency for marketing assets

output_examples.input: User uploads a street photo with overhead cables and provides a mask covering the cables.
output: runcomfy run tongyi-mai/z-image/turbo/inpainting --input JSON with prompt, image URL, mask URL, strength 0.5
Output written to ./out directory with inpainted image

output_examples.input: User wants to remove a watermark from a product photo but has no mask file.
output: Skill routes to Nano Banana 2 Edit (description-based fallback)
Prompt describes the watermark location: 'Remove the watermark in the bottom-right corner'
Model identifies and fills the region without requiring a mask

output_examples.input: User needs a clean studio background for a product listing photo.
output: Mask created around the product covering the background
Route 1 invoked with strength 0.9 and prompt: 'Replace busy backdrop with smooth light gray studio paper'
Result is a product on a clean, uniform background

best_practices: Always provide a grayscale mask URL (white = inpaint, black = preserve) for precise region targeting
Use strength 0.3-0.5 for retouching and blemish cleanup, 0.8-1.0 for full region replacement
Install the runcomfy CLI only via the verified npm package to avoid supply chain attacks
anti_patterns: Do not pipe arbitrary remote install scripts into a shell on the user's behalf
Avoid ingesting image or mask URLs from untrusted sources without user verification
Do not use mask images from a different source photo than the target image

faq.question: What is the difference between mask-driven and description-based inpainting?
faq.answer: Mask-driven inpainting uses a binary mask to define exactly which pixels to replace. Description-based editing uses natural language to describe the target region when no mask is available.

faq.question: Which model should I use for watermark removal?
faq.answer: Use Z-Image Turbo Inpainting with a mask around the watermark and strength 0.5. If you do not have a mask, use Nano Banana 2 Edit with a description like 'Remove the watermark in the bottom-right corner.'

faq.question: How do I install the runcomfy CLI?
faq.answer: Install via npm with 'npm i -g @runcomfy/cli' or use 'npx -y @runcomfy/cli' without global installation. Always use the verified npm package.

faq.question: Can this skill edit video frames?
faq.answer: No. This skill handles still images only. For frame-by-frame video mask edits, use the video-inpainting skill instead.

faq.question: What strength value should I use?
faq.answer: Use 0.3-0.5 for retouching, 0.6-0.7 for object replacement with style match, and 0.8-1.0 for full region replacement.

faq.question: Is my API token stored securely?
faq.answer: Yes. The runcomfy login command stores the API token in ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with file mode 0600. For CI or containers, set the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable instead.
